content:
2 min read
Just now

--

Today, I attended my first class of the Python Crash Course at CodeBettle, and it was a wonderful experience. The session began with an introductory overview of CodeBettle, where we learned about their mission of providing quality education and practical learning opportunities for students who want to build strong technical skills.
Our instructor, Sir Muhammad Hateem Hussain, introduced himself and shared that he has been teaching for the past four years. His experience and teaching approach made the session engaging and motivating. Sir Zohad Ijaz was also present throughout the class, guiding us and ensuring that everyone felt comfortable and involved.
One of the most interactive parts of the session was the student introductions. Each participant introduced themselves by sharing their name, educational background (Medical or Non-Medical), the degree they are pursuing, and the university they are studying at. It was a great opportunity to know our classmates and learn about their diverse academic backgrounds.
Both instructors guided us with patience and encouraged us to stay consistent throughout the course. Their positive attitude created a welcoming learning environment and increased our confidence for the journey ahead.
As a BS Software Engineering student, I am excited to begin learning Python through this crash course. I hope this program will strengthen my programming skills, improve my problem-solving abilities, and help me grow as a future software engineer.
I’m looking forward to the upcoming sessions and the knowledge they will bring.
InShaAllah, this course will be a valuable step toward excelling in Python and achieving my learning goals.
